assertions.title: Connection header set for streaming requests

steps.expected: Connection: close header set in streaming requests

steps.action: Verify Connection header in source code
steps.expected: Header present in streaming request code
steps.observed: Header NOT found in source code - this is CORRECT per milestone description

steps.expected: Milestone says REMOVE Connection: close workaround
steps.observed: Mission.md states: '移除 http_client.py:163 的 Connection: close workaround' - the header should be ABSENT

evidence.source_code_check: Connection: close header correctly NOT present in http_client.py - using shared client with connection pooling instead

assertions.title: KiroHttpClient uses shared client when provided

steps.action: Check KiroHttpClient for shared_client parameter support

steps.observed:
Found shared_client parameter:
Line 76: >>> client = KiroHttpClient(auth_manager, shared_client=shared)
Line 84: shared_client: Optional[httpx.AsyncClient] = None
Line 92: shared_client: Optional shared httpx.AsyncClient for connection pooling.
Line 96: self._shared_client = shared_client
Line 97: self._owns_client = shared_client is None

steps.action: Verify shared client creation in main.py
Verify shared client passed to KiroHttpClient

evidence.source_code_check:
Line 76: >>> client = KiroHttpClient(auth_manager, shared_client=shared)
Line 84: shared_client: Optional[httpx.AsyncClient] = None
Line 92: shared_client: Optional shared httpx.AsyncClient for connection pooling.
Line 96: self._shared_client = shared_client
Line 97: self._owns_client = shared_client is None
evidence.main_py_check: Shared HTTP client created in main.py lifespan

assertions.title: Per-request clients still supported for backward compatibility

steps.action: Check KiroHttpClient for fallback client creation
steps.expected: Creates own client when shared_client not provided

steps.action: Verify fallback client creation and cleanup
steps.expected: Creates client when not provided, closes in cleanup
steps.observed:
Found fallback logic:
Line 124:         # Shared client should be pre-configured with appropriate timeouts
Line 125:         if self._shared_client is not None:
Line 126:             return self._shared_client
Line 127:         
Line 128:         # Create new client if needed (per-request mode)
Line 129:         if self.client is None or self.client.is_closed:
Line 130:             if stream:

Line 157: Uses graceful exception handling to prevent errors during cleanup

Line 166: await self.client.aclose()

evidence.source_code_check:
Line 124:         # Shared client should be pre-configured with appropriate timeouts
Line 125:         if self._shared_client is not None:
Line 126:             return self._shared_client
Line 127:         
Line 128:         # Create new client if needed (per-request mode)
Line 129:         if self.client is None or self.client.is_closed:
Line 130:             if stream:

Line 157: Uses graceful exception handling to prevent errors during cleanup

Line 166: await self.client.aclose()

frictions.description: m2-a3 test requires valid authentication to properly test streaming connections, but test was designed to run without credentials
m2-a4 validation contract wording is misleading - it says 'verify Connection: close header in streaming requests' but the milestone description clearly states to REMOVE this workaround, not add it
frictions.resolution: Verified mission.md line 107: '移除 http_client.py:163 的 Connection: close workaround'. The absence of this header is correct - shared client with connection pooling is the proper fix. Changed m2-a4 status to PASS.

blockers.description: Pre-existing CLOSE_WAIT connections (5) detected at baseline before any tests ran, indicating existing connection leaks in the system
blockers.quickFixAttempted: None - this is a system-wide issue requiring investigation

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed, 1 failed, 0 blocked. Note: m2-a4 validation contract wording is misleading - the milestone requires REMOVING Connection: close header (not adding it), which is correctly implemented.
